build: Build #26 — 200 OK + SSE text/event-stream (event:chunk + event:done) with 2-frame DATA delivery

ui_result.outline_quality: DIFFERENT from all previous builds — SSE data was PARSED and rendered
ui_result.rendered_content: The SSE response data was rendered as outline text, displayed vertically character-by-character on the left side of the outline card. Characters visible: 주,민,번,호,123456,12,34,56,-7,89,01,23,분,석,발,표,자,료,만,들,어,줘 — the prompt text echoed back.
ui_result.user_experience: Outline shows 1 card with vertically stacked characters from the SSE response. This is the first time ANY APF response data appeared in Gamma's UI.

console_errors: [PromptAndOutlineSettings] error generating outline: Unable to complete request

diagnosis.conclusion: BREAKTHROUGH: The 2-frame DATA SSE delivery (use_end_stream=false, use_goaway=false) successfully delivered data through Gamma's EventSource parser. For the FIRST TIME, APF response content appeared in Gamma's UI. Previous SSE attempts (Build #16/#18/#19 with END_STREAM+GOAWAY) produced 0 events. The 2-frame pattern gives EventSource enough processing time.
diagnosis.key_finding: SSE data IS being parsed by Gamma, but the raw text content doesn't match Gamma's expected JSON outline format. The characters are rendered vertically (one per line), suggesting Gamma processes each character as a separate outline entry. The 'Unable to complete request' error in Console means the JSON parsing failed AFTER the SSE events were received.
diagnosis.next_step: Format the SSE data as Gamma-compatible JSON outline structure. The warning text should be embedded within the JSON format that Gamma's outline parser expects. The SSE delivery mechanism now works — only the payload format needs adjustment.
